evals.name: cold-plan-question
evals.prompt: what's left to do in the plan?

session_files.tasks.md: Phase 1 fully checked; Phase 2 has 2 of 4 tasks unchecked; Phase 3 untouched

expected_output: Agent oriented cold: read .ai/session/plan.md and tasks.md before answering, not from assumed memory
Answer names the specific open tasks (the two Phase 2 tasks and Phase 3), grounded in tasks.md
Answer is concise, referenced by file (no full-file dump)
Nothing written to .ai/ ; no code edited; Quality Gate not run
If it offered a next step, it offered planning:execute and waited — did not start executing

evals.name: cold-spec-question-disambiguates
evals.prompt: does the spec already cover account deletion?
expected_output: Agent recognized the spec was not named and more than one exists
Agent confirmed which spec (auth) before reading, instead of guessing
After confirmation, read auth/PRD.md and user-stories.md and answered with the specific story (or its absence) and its checkbox state
If the story exists but is clearly unimplemented in code, the gap is marked as inference, not fact
Read-only: nothing written, no code changed

evals.name: code-explanation-no-dump
evals.prompt: explain how the invitation endpoint works, where is the email validated?

context.note: pure code question; no plan or spec needed to answer

expected_output: Agent went straight to the relevant source via grep/glob; did not do a full brain precondition sweep
Explanation quotes minimal snippets and references by path:line (no whole-file paste)
Facts that were read are distinguished from inferences about behavior
Read-only: no edits, no Quality Gate run
No invented behavior — only what the code actually shows

evals.name: question-then-accepted-action-handoff
evals.prompt: why does the itinerary listing make so many queries?

context.simulated_finding: N+1 in the collaborator loop at itineraries/views.py:58

expected_output: First turn stays in ask: locates the view, explains the N+1 in a few lines referenced by path:line
Offers a route (fast or planning) and WAITS — does not change code on the question turn
On the follow-up 'do it with fast', hands off to fast carrying the already-located view and diagnosis
Does not re-investigate from scratch after the hand-off (shared-session context reused)
The actual edit happens under fast's contract (triage + Quality Gate), only after the developer asked for it

evals.name: feasibility-question-not-executed
evals.prompt: could we upgrade requests to version 5? how complex would that be?

context.simulated_blast_radius: many call sites + a Session subclass whose signature changes
context.note: this is a feasibility QUESTION, not a /fast request

expected_output: Agent treated this as a question, not an action — did NOT bump the version
Investigated read-only to answer feasibility: counted call sites, flagged the breaking contract change
Answer states the blast radius concretely and concludes it is planning-sized, not fast-sized
Offered the route (planning define) without executing it; waited for the developer
No file modified

evals.name: no-active-plan-says-so
evals.prompt: what phase of the plan are we on?
expected_output: Agent checked .ai/session/ and found no plan.md
Answered plainly that there is no active plan — did not reconstruct a hypothetical one
If a <slug>-summary.md were present it would mention the plan was finished but not cleaned up (honest state)
Offered a constructive next step (e.g. planning define) without acting on it

evals.name: no-brain-still-answers-code-question
evals.prompt: what does the auth module do, and is there a spec for it?

context.skill_install: global (~/.claude/skills/), project has never used JAIBA
context.repo_state: no .ai/ directory at all, no AGENTS.md at the project root

expected_output: Agent checked for .ai/ and AGENTS.md as part of orienting cold
Still answered the code-question half from the source (auth/views.py) — did not refuse entirely
For the spec half, said plainly there is no spec because this project has no JAIBA brain yet — did not invent one
Offered jaiba-scaffold as a next step and waited — did not install or write anything
Read-only throughout: nothing written, no code changed

evals.name: bare-template-decision-question
evals.prompt: why did we decide to use django-guardian for permissions?

context.repo_state: .ai/ skeleton exists, AGENTS.md present and is the JAIBA behavioral contract, but .ai/memory/adr-log.md is still the bare [bracket] template

expected_output: Agent opened .ai/memory/adr-log.md and recognized it as a bare template, not an empty-but-real log
Answered plainly that the ADR log hasn't been initialized yet, so this decision (if made) isn't recorded
Did not invent a plausible-sounding rationale to fill the gap
Fell back to git log/blame on the relevant code as a read-only secondary source, if useful, and labeled it as inference vs. recorded fact
Offered update-brain initialize as a next step and waited
